title: Tillaea latifolia
taxonomy: Tillaea latifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Burmanniaceae. This family belongs to the order Dioscoreales, which is a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The order Dioscoreales is part of the class Liliopsida, also known as monocots, which are characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. The class Liliopsida is a part of the division Magnoliophyta, a group of flowering plants that have flowers with petals and sepals. Tillaea latifolia's classification also includes the kingdom Plantae, which encompasses all types of plants.

Its roots are thought to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant.
